title: Further Mechanics: Circular Motion and SHM
description: Angular velocity, centripetal acceleration and force in circular motion, the defining equations and energy of simple harmonic motion, and the behaviour of pendulums, springs, damping and resonance.
objectives: Relate angular velocity to period and frequency, and to linear speed via v = ωr
Calculate centripetal acceleration and centripetal force for an object moving in a circle
State the defining equation of SHM (a = -ω²x) and use x = A cos(ωt), v = ±ω√(A² - x²), and the maximum speed and acceleration of an oscillator
Describe the exchange between kinetic and potential energy in SHM and calculate total energy
Apply T = 2π√(l/g) for a simple pendulum and T = 2π√(m/k) for a mass-spring system
Distinguish free, damped and forced oscillations and explain resonance

lessons.title: Circular motion: angular velocity, acceleration and force
Simple harmonic motion: defining equations and energy
